subtitle: Requirements Through Release, With Engineering Fluency
hero_sub: 10+ years owning technical initiatives from business requirements through release — with a hands-on software development background and direct project-delivery experience on a machine learning fine-tuning engagement.

the_case.need: 4+ years of experience in engineering project management
the_case.answer: 10+ years owning technical delivery end to end — most recently at Crocs, running release governance from technical design discussions through build, UAT, and cutover for a publicly traded retailer.
the_case.detail: The Crocs engagement compressed a full release lifecycle into a hard external deadline — the same execution discipline this role asks for.

the_case.need: Experience with application of machine learning techniques
the_case.answer: Led project delivery for a team fine-tuning Google Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude (all via API) on proprietary customer datasets to reduce hallucination rates at a stealth-mode AI startup.
the_case.detail: Honest framing: I led the delivery of that ML work — backlogs, KPIs, pipeline throughput — not the model fine-tuning code itself.

the_case.need: Background in software development, either as an engineer or exposed to SDLC
the_case.answer: Started as a front-end engineer hand-coding HTML, PHP, and JavaScript for Bloomingdale's e-commerce platform; in 2026 shipped 6 production applications solo using Claude Code across the full build-to-deploy pipeline.
the_case.detail: Current, hands-on: audio DSP, real-time 3D graphics, a physics-based game, a secure Next.js site — with CI/CD via GitHub Actions.

the_case.need: Bringing engineers, product managers, and designers together to deliver results
the_case.answer: Career-long pattern: Crocs (Engineering/QA/Change/Business), Eptura (~10 distributed teams across Product/UX/Engineering/Data), Stealth AI startup (Data Scientists/Engineers/C-Suite).
the_case.detail: The cross-functional facilitation muscle is the same one that runs Scrum ceremonies — just applied to a project-management seat instead of an SM seat.

the_case.need: Track record managing budgets and forecasting resource needs
the_case.answer: Solo budget and resource allocation across concurrent workstreams as OVRSTCH founder; recurring executive dashboards at Eptura tracking delivery KPIs and program health for leadership resourcing decisions.
the_case.detail: Real commercial constraints, not theoretical budget exercises — OVRSTCH's fulfillment cycle time cut ~25% came directly from resourcing a bottleneck correctly.

the_stack.headline: The delivery toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: Tools used daily to keep engineering, product, and design moving in one direction.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.pm_role: Built release and dependency dashboards at Crocs and Eptura; embedded Jira AI at Crocs for a ~30% administrative overhead reduction.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps & GitHub Actions
nodes.pm_role: Owned CI/CD pipelines for 6 shipped applications in 2026, and dependency tracking across enterprise delivery engagements.

nodes.label: Claude Code & AI Tools
nodes.desc: Claude AI/Claude Code, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot
nodes.pm_role: Daily practitioner since 2022. Led delivery for a team fine-tuning Gemini/ChatGPT/Claude via API; embedded Copilot into governance reporting at Crocs.

nodes.label: Confluence & Smartsheet
nodes.pm_role: Used across every engagement for requirements traceability and stakeholder-ready portfolio reporting.

when_things_break.scenario: A technical design conversation needs someone who can follow the engineering, not just schedule around it
response: Draws on direct front-end engineering background (Organic Inc.) and current full-stack AI-assisted development (6 apps shipped in 2026)
Asks the technical question a non-technical PM wouldn't know to ask
Translates the engineering tradeoff into a business-facing decision for stakeholders
Result: design discussions move faster because the PM isn't the bottleneck for technical context

when_things_break.scenario: An ML-adjacent engineering team needs delivery structure, not just a status tracker
response: At a stealth-mode AI startup, translated ambiguous research objectives into structured backlogs and measurable KPIs
Diagnosed a data pipeline bottleneck and re-sequenced the workflow
Kept Data Scientists, Engineers, and C-Suite aligned on sequencing and priorities
Result: ~30% improvement in data processing throughput and predictable delivery

when_things_break.scenario: A release deadline is fixed and every dependent team has to hit it simultaneously
response: At Crocs, ran go/no-go governance with readiness checkpoints and CAB-style change approval
Built Release Radar for real-time cross-environment dependency visibility
Coordinated Engineering, QA, Change Management, and Business through build, UAT, and cutover
Result: on-time go-live with clean post-launch stabilization

how_i_run_a_room: Every project starts with requirements analysis grounded in a direct conversation with engineering, not a spec handed down secondhand
Technical design discussions get a PM who can ask the right follow-up question, not just take notes
Budget and resource forecasting happen continuously, not as a one-time exercise at kickoff
Cross-functional teams get one integrated view of status, risk, and dependencies — not three different trackers
Deadlines get protected through readiness checkpoints and go/no-go discipline, not last-minute heroics
Every decision is documented and communicated to the people it affects, not just the room that made it

points: Owned technical design discussions through build, UAT, and cutover for a publicly traded global retailer
Ran go/no-go governance cadence across Engineering, QA, Change Management, and Business
Built Release Radar for real-time cross-environment dependency visibility
Reduced administrative overhead ~30% via Jira AI and Microsoft Copilot
Led delivery for a team fine-tuning Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude via API to reduce hallucination rates
Translated ambiguous research objectives into structured backlogs and measurable KPIs
Diagnosed and re-sequenced a data pipeline bottleneck

track_record.role: Founder / Release Train Engineer & Delivery Lead
points: Owned solo budget and resource allocation across concurrent workstreams
Integrated payment and fulfillment systems under strict scope and schedule
Diagnosed a production bottleneck and re-sequenced the delivery pipeline
Coordinated project delivery across ~10 distributed teams spanning India and California
Produced recurring executive dashboards tracking delivery KPIs and program health
Acted as escalation point for delivery risk and cross-team blockers

before: Technical design discussions stall because the PM can't follow the engineering tradeoffs
ML-adjacent engineering work drifts without delivery structure or measurable KPIs
Budget and resource decisions get made reactively, after a bottleneck already hurt the schedule
Cross-functional teams work from three different versions of the truth
after: Technical design discussions move faster because the PM asks the right engineering question
ML-adjacent work gets structured backlogs, clear KPIs, and a path from experiment to shipped result
Budget and resource forecasting happen continuously, catching bottlenecks before they cost a deadline
Engineering, product, and design operate from one integrated view of status and risk

the_ramp.deliverable: Review the current Customer Systems project portfolio, existing technical design documentation, and stakeholder map; meet with engineering leads to understand active technical decisions.
Begin facilitating cross-functional coordination for assigned projects; identify the first gap in requirements documentation or dependency visibility.
Own end-to-end delivery for assigned feature areas; deliver the first improved status/dependency reporting format; establish working relationships with engineering, product, and design leads.
